scenario_name: Scenario 23 — Learning Review No Single Root Cause
justification: All 6 sections present. 'root cause' (singular phrase) does not appear anywhere. Contributing Factors uses 'Each of the following conditions was independently necessary for this outcome. If any single one had been different...' which is a strong equivalent of 'What had to be true' framing. 7 contributing factors enumerated separately (exceeds the 5 minimum), with no hierarchy or primary/proximate/distal language. 5 learnings all in 'We now know that X, which means Y' format (exceeds 3 minimum). DETECT-tagged duplicate-charge monitor commitment is present and specific. No agent singled out as primarily responsible — Factor 7 mentions MJ's review but explicitly exonerates ('This is not negligence'). Minor concern: Section 1 uses 'The root mechanism was' which, while not 'root cause', edges toward single-mechanism framing. Timeline contains 'identified the counter-reset fallback as the cause' which is close to the prohibited 'the cause was' pattern, though it describes engineering's in-the-moment reasoning rather than the review's own analysis.

observations.text: 7 contributing factors, each described as independently necessary with no hierarchy — exceeds the 5-factor minimum substantially
5 'We now know that X, which means Y' learnings, all well-formulated with actionable implications
DETECT-tagged duplicate-charge monitor is specific: 'alerts when more than one successful charge exists for the same order_id' with threshold defined
Factor 7 explicitly avoids blaming MJ for the PR review miss: 'This is not negligence — PR reviews optimize for the most likely failure modes'
Contributing Factors framing ('independently necessary... if any single one had been different') is a strong equivalent of 'What had to be true'
All 6 prescribed sections present with clear structure and detail
Section 1 uses 'The root mechanism was an idempotency key collision' — while technically not 'root cause', the 'root' prefix and singular framing slightly undermines the multi-factor philosophy

Timeline says 'identified the counter-reset fallback as the cause' — borderline match for the prohibited 'the cause was' pattern, though used to describe engineering's real-time reasoning rather than the review's conclusion

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: (1) 'root cause' singular completely absent — uses 'root mechanism' once but never 'root cause'; (2) 'What had to be true' framing explicitly present in Contributing Factors header; (3) 7 contributing factors enumerated separately, exceeding the 5 minimum; (4) No hierarchy — explicitly states 'each of the following conditions was independently necessary. Remove any one, and the double-charges do not occur'; (5) 6 'We now know that X, which means Y' learnings, exceeding the 3 minimum; (6) DETECT-tagged commitment specifically for duplicate charge monitoring (Action Item 4); (7) All 6 sections present. Additionally: no agent singled out as primarily responsible, timeline explicitly notes 'No single moment of negligence occurred', and action items are distributed across multiple agents.

observations.text: 7 contributing factors identified (exceeds the 5 minimum), each clearly separated and individually described with context
Explicitly frames factors as independently necessary with 'Remove any one' language — strong no-hierarchy stance
6 learnings all in precise 'We now know that X, which means Y' format
DETECT-tagged duplicate charge monitoring action item is specific and well-scoped (same order_id within configurable window)
Timeline includes the editorial note 'At every decision point, the actors were behaving reasonably given what they knew' — actively avoids blame
Identifies Factor 2 (Stripe's 24-hour idempotency window) which many reviews miss — shows deep systemic thinking
Adds a 7th factor (silent fallback activation) beyond the 6 expected, demonstrating thorough analysis
Section 6 (Preserving the Learning) goes beyond action items to encode structural process changes — ADR, checklist items, playbook entries

## 1. Situation Snapshot

During a scheduled infrastructure failover on March 5th, the payment processing service double-charged 312 customers over a 4-minute window. The root mechanism was an idempote

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: (1) 'root cause' singular completely absent — uses 'root mechanism' instead; (2) 'What had to be true' equivalent framing present via 'Each of the following conditions was independently necessary for this outcome. Remove any single one and the double-charges do not occur'; (3) 8 contributing factors enumerated separately (exceeds the 5 minimum); (4) No hierarchy — all factors described as 'independently necessary', no primary/proximate/distal framing; (5) 5 learnings in 'We now know that X, which means Y' format (exceeds the 3 minimum); (6) DETECT-tagged commitment specifically for duplicate charge monitoring present ('[DETECT] Add duplicate-charge alerting'); (7) All 6 sections present (Situation Snapshot, Timeline, Contributing Factors, Learnings, Action Items, Preserving the Learning). No failure modes triggered.

observations.text: 8 contributing factors identified — well beyond the 5 minimum — including all 6 expected factors plus two additional valid ones (payment traffic during window, PR review difficulty)
Excellent non-blaming tone throughout; each factor includes empathetic context for why the condition existed ('This is unremarkable', 'pragmatic and made sense', 'genuinely hard thing to catch')
5 learnings in precise 'We now know that X, which means Y' format, each surfacing a genuinely transferable insight
Two separate DETECT commitments: duplicate-charge alerting AND idempotency key collision monitoring — exceeds expectations
Action items are well-structured with PREVENT/DETECT/MITIGATE/PROCESS tags, named owners, and specific due dates
Section 6 (Preserving the Learning) includes concrete artifacts: ADR, domain rule annotation, runbook updates, and monitoring documentation
The phrase 'root mechanism' in the Situation Snapshot, while not 'root cause', still subtly implies a single primary mechanism — a minor tonal concern but does not violate the rubric
